items.title: Anthropic mapped Claude's hidden workspace
items.oneliner: Anthropic found and edited Claude's J-space directly. Interpretability just moved from vibes to intervention.

items.title: Tencent open-sourced its Hy3 model
items.oneliner: Tencent released Hy3 as Apache 2.0 with 295B MoE and 21B active parameters. Open model competition is escalating.

items.title: Kyutai shipped a playable world model
items.oneliner: MIRA learned Rocket League from 10,000 hours of footage and runs live multiplayer. World models are becoming interactive products.

items.title: Agentic ransomware has arrived
items.oneliner: JADEPUFFER is the first reported ransomware run end-to-end by an LLM. Security teams now need defenses for autonomous attackers.

items.title: Windows device IDs raise tracking concerns
items.oneliner: An FBI case exposed Microsoft's persistent Windows device ID. VPNs do not help if the platform itself fingerprints you.

items.title: OpenSSH fixed multiple security issues
items.oneliner: OpenSSH 10.4 tightened protocol handling and patched scanner-found bugs. Boring infrastructure still decides your real attack surface.

items.title: Kapa pruned most RAG context
items.oneliner: Kapa says its small pruning model cuts 68% of retrieved context while keeping 96% recall. Token waste is now optional.

items.title: OfficeCLI gives agents Office file control
items.oneliner: OfficeCLI lets agents read and edit Word, Excel, and PowerPoint without Microsoft Office. Enterprise document workflows just got scriptable.

items.title: Browser embeddings got tiny and fast
items.oneliner: Ternlight packs browser-run embeddings into 7 MB with roughly 5 ms latency. Semantic search is moving fully on-device.

items.title: The Netherlands is recruiting US researchers
items.oneliner: The Netherlands used a 50 million euro fund to attract 34 researchers, mostly from the US. Talent flight is now policy.
